findings.description: Substrate-path bug fix mid-execution unblocked all future panel runs. Found during W1's panel run that bin/guild derive-panel looked at .claude/agents/PANEL-COMPOSITION.md while the spec lived at agents/PANEL-COMPOSITION.md. Fixed standalone (moved to docs/PANEL-COMPOSITION.md), and from W2 onward the auto-derived panels just worked.
Free-polish-from-evaluator pattern: every approved checkin applied at least one advisory finding inline as polish. The evaluator panel was net-positive contribution per unit, not just a gate.
findings.evidence: InitAction→InitResult in W2 (b7ea2de); parallel-safety test rename + scaffolding drop in W3 (5d5c1d4); missing-node test.skipIf in W6 (9a6e4df); V11 walker fence-state bug in W4 (af360e7)

findings.description: Contract-first sub-step ordering in W2: amendments → registry → body → dispatch all in one commit, but ordered so the registry tripwire would catch a missing amendment. Tripwire-as-design forced correct shape without splitting into 4 sub-commits.

PR #11 grew to 13 commits before the split-into-N option was surfaced. Should have proposed the split around commit 8-9 when the diff first started feeling overwhelming. The W3 unit was a natural fork point that wasn't recognized at the time.
findings.evidence: PR #11 was split into PR #11 (W0-W6) + PR #12 (W7-W9) mid-execution

findings.description: Relative-path arithmetic recurred after git mv-ing files between dirs. Two corrections in this project shared the same shape: 'moves between dirs trip relative paths AND in-file references to the thing-just-moved/deleted.' Smoke tests caught both; a pre-move audit would have caught them earlier.
findings.evidence: W7 correction: ../lib/ → ../../lib/ inside moved loom verbs + fixture paths in moved tests (e00c808). W9 correction: .gitignore comment still naming install.sh after the file was deleted (c6b58e0).

findings.description: W0's generator-tree size estimate was ~2× the actual. Estimated ~908 KB / ~125 files; W7's real sync produced ~452 KB / 69 files. W0 counted all .ts files; the sync rule excludes test files. PLAN's R6 (review cognitive load) was sized against the wrong number. Future migrations should size against the actual sync rule, not raw file counts.
findings.evidence: W0 checkin 01 vs W7 commit e00c808 actual sync output

findings.description: PR-splitting mid-migration is a legitimate move when the unified PR grows unreviewable. PLAN's PQ1 ('one bundled PR') is a design-time call; if the diff grows too large, the agent should surface the split-into-N option without unilateral action. Re-ask PQ1 around the 8-10 commit mark on any 'one bundled PR' decision.
findings.evidence: Captured as feedback_pq1_bundled_pr_revisitable.md in user-memory; the deviation rationale lives in PR #11's body as 'Part 1 of 2 ... PQ1 is the original framing; the actual split into two PRs is named as the lived deviation'

findings.description: After-merge phase-completed bookkeeping doesn't have a clean home in the current substrate. Running `loom phase update --status=completed --pr-state=merged` post-merge leaves manifest+events dirty in the working tree with no obvious commit destination. Options worth exploring: a `loom project archive --include-bookkeeping` flag (which is the de-facto path right now); a formal 'post-merge cleanup' commit type.
findings.evidence: This retro's archive run is the de-facto home — manifest+events updates from `loom phase update --status=completed` ride along with `loom project archive`

findings.description: V6 + V7 smoke tests post-merge: verify `claude plugin install agent-loop-full@krambuhl --scope user` works end-to-end on personal Mac (V6) and Patreon machine (V7). V7 includes the load-bearing colleague-invisibility check that W0 V1's --scope user recommendation rests on.

CI hookup of `scripts/sync-shared.ts --check`. PLAN named this as W7 scope but the wiring (GitHub Action or pre-commit hook) was deferred. Worth doing before the next significant cli/ edit, otherwise drift could land silently.

V8 public-publish gating decision: whether V6/V7 run before or after the repo goes public is an open question in PLAN. Operationally important — testing in private gives a safety buffer; testing post-public gives the real install path. Decide before announcing.
